name: Priscilla's Tofu Pot Pie:
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the tofu into 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Mix the flour, salt, pepper, and garlic powder in a bowl.
3. Add the tofu cubes to the dry mixture and toss until coated.
4. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
5. Cook the tofu cubes until golden and crisp, stirring occasionally for about 10 minutes.
6. Add the chopped onion and continue cooking for another 3 minutes.
7. Stir in carrots, celery, peas, and water. Cover and cook on medium heat until the vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes.
8. Remove from heat and stir in half of the gravy mixture.
9. For the gravy: Melt margerine in a saucepan over medium heat. Add mushrooms and cook until soft, stirring occasionally for about 5-7 minutes.
10. Stir in flour and seasonings and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes.
11. Whisk in water and simmer uncovered until thickened, about 10 minutes.
12. Mix the remaining gravy into the tofu mixture and set aside.
13. For the crust: Combine flour, wheat germ, and salt. Add margerine and cold water to form a dough. Divide into two pieces.
14. Roll out one piece of dough on a floured surface and place it in a 10-inch pie plate.
15. Roll out the remaining dough and set aside for later use.
16. Spread tofu and vegetable mixture evenly over the bottom crust, leaving some space at the edges for topping.
17. Pour the remaining gravy over the filling.
18. Cover with the top crust and fold the edges of both crusts together.
19. Pinch the edges to seal and make slits in the top crust for steam to escape.
20. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 20 minutes, then reduce temperature to 350°F (175°C) and bake an additional 20-30 minutes until the crust is nicely browned.
Hints:
If there is too much gravy for your casserole pan, serve it over individual pieces as you serve the pot pie. This recipe serves 8 to 10 people.
